violations.text: The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution dispensed by the wall dispenser of the 3-bay sink was less than 200 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 - 400 ppm. At the time of inspection, staff switched to using Quaternary Ammonium tablets for proper sanitizing of equipment and utensils. Technician to be in next day to properly adjust. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Several items in the Prep Unit were observed at 44-45 degrees Fahrenheit. Per the PIC, the items were recently stocked from the walk-in cooler. Food items were moved back to the walk-in cooler to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

The following equipment was observed in need of repair/adjustment... - The Prep Unit was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 43 degrees Fahrenheit with food items observed at 44-45 degrees Fahrenheit (on ice slurry). Per the PIC, technician is due same day as inspection for repairs to the prep unit. - The wall mounted sanitizer dispenser at the 3 bay sink was observed not dispensing the proper amount of sanitizer for proper sanitizing. Technician due on-site next day for adjustments. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The following areas of the floors/walls/ceiling were observed in need of repair/adjustment... - Corners/joints of wall boards were observed to be loosened with gaps - Several floor tiles in the kitchen and service areas were observed to be cracked/chipped and in poor repair. - The wooden stand below the mops ink is need of sealing/resealing (exposed wood) The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2026

Several floor tiles in the kitchen and service areas were observed to be cracked/chipped and in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026
